_meta.method: Web research across primary org sites (zenbuddhism.ie, zenireland.com, greyheronzen.ie, corkzen.ie, zengalway.ie, OneDropZen Dublin Meetup) and pan-European directories (kwanumeurope.org, plumvillage.org via Mindfulness Ireland sangha list, zen-azi.org). Filtered to Republic of Ireland only; Northern Ireland (Black Mountain Zen Belfast) excluded for the UK file. Excluded Triratna/Dublin Buddhist Centre (not Zen lineage) and generic mindfulness sanghas.
_meta.coverageEstimate: High (~90%) for established Zen dojos/centres; Plum Village sangha list reflects publicly registered groups via Mindfulness Ireland (Plum Village Ireland's umbrella).
knownGaps: No Kwan Um (Mu Sang Sa) presence found in Ireland as of 2026 — prompt's Cork reference appears outdated; not listed on kwanumeurope.org.
No Western Chan Fellowship local group confirmed in Ireland.
No Sanbo Zen affiliate confirmed in Ireland.
Several Plum Village sanghas list a contact-only address; some meet rotationally and may not have a fixed venue.

places.name: Dublin Zen Centre (Zen Buddhism Ireland)
places.lineage: Sōtō (Nishijima Roshi lineage; teacher Rev. Myōzan Kōdō Kilroy, dharma heir of Taigu Turlur)
places.address: 11 Anglesea Street, Temple Bar, Dublin, D02 NY01
places.notes: Ireland's first dedicated Zen centre, opened April 2022. Registered charity 20155384. Soto Zen Buddhist Association affiliate.

places.name: Earth+Sky Zen — Dublin Dojo
places.lineage: Sōtō / Deshimaru (AZI) and White Plum Asanga (teacher Soshin Mary Laheen Roshi; dharma transmission from Robert Jinsen Kennedy Roshi 2019)
places.notes: Dublin Dojo founded 1996 by Alain Tainan Liebmann (Deshimaru lineage, AZI). Now under Mary Laheen Roshi (Roshi 2025); spans AZI/Sotoshu and White Plum Asanga.

places.name: Grey Heron Zen
places.lineage: White Plum Asanga (teacher Miriam Healy Roshi, dharma transmission from Robert Kennedy Roshi 2014); also Zen Peacemaker Order
places.address: Dominican Retreat House, Tallaght Village, Dublin 24
places.notes: Active in Dublin since September 2011. Co-founded with Mary McGrane Sensei. Joint sesshins with Earth+Sky Zendo.

places.name: OneDropZen Dublin
places.lineage: Rinzai (One Drop Zen / Sogenji line; Shōdō Harada Rōshi, Hokuozan Sogenji Europe)
places.notes: Weekly Thursday zazen 8:15pm. Affiliated to Hokuozan Sogenji (Germany), the European hub of Harada Roshi's One Drop Zen sangha.

places.name: Cork Zen Dojo
places.notes: Founded by Alain Tainan Liebmann after 1991. Core group running monthly zazen since 2012; visiting AZI teachers.

places.name: Galway Zen Dojo
places.address: Áras na nGael, 45 Dominick Street, Galway
places.notes: Founded 1993 by Alain Tainan Liebmann. Currently led by Tom Jikai Cleary. Weekly zazen plus annual Aran Islands summer camp; sesshins at Esker Monastery.

places.name: Open Heart Sangha
places.address: Oscailt Integrative Health Centre, 8 Pembroke Road, Ballsbridge, Dublin 4
places.notes: Tuesdays 19:30–21:00. Listed on Mindfulness Ireland (Plum Village Ireland umbrella).

places.name: Wake-Up Dublin
places.lineage: Plum Village (Thich Nhat Hanh; Wake Up young-adult network)
places.address: Oscailt Integrative Health Centre, 8 Pembroke Road, Ballsbridge, Dublin 4
places.notes: Sundays 19:00–21:00. Part of the global Wake Up sangha network.

places.name: Sonas Sangha (Mindfulness for Children)
places.notes: Family/children's sangha; first Sunday of each month 14:00–16:00.

places.name: The Peace of Wild Things Family Sangha
places.address: Ballincollig Regional Park, Ballincollig, Co. Cork
places.notes: Outdoor family sangha; second Sunday of each month.

places.name: Solas Gheal Sangha
places.address: Loughrea Family Resource Centre, Loughrea, H53 E891

places.name: Full Moon Sangha
places.address: Gorse Hill, Cliff Road, Windgates, Greystones, Co. Wicklow
